label: plain_text
text:
Here,  CAB
 (or, in brief, angle A) is an
acute angle. Note the position of the side BC
with respect to angle A. It faces Z A. We call it
the side opposite to angle A. AC is the
hypotenuse of the right triangle and the side AB
is a part of Z A. So, we call it the side
adjacent to angle A.
